text:
The Olympic Movement Breaks were created by the Olympic Federation of Ireland's Dare to Believe schools’ programme to be used as part of the Department of Education’s Active School Flag programme.

RTÉ's news2day caught up with Olympian Thomas Barr on his visit to St Fergal's BNS in Dublin to show them how it's done! Just press play on the image up top.

Teachers can sign up for the prgrammehere. And, you can see lots more resources and fun activities on the officialDare To Believe website.If you hurry, you can even invite a top athleet to come for a visit.

There is a special resource pack for schools too -click here to download it now!

Olympians, Paralympians and high-performance athletes across twelve sports including athletics, boxing, breakdancing, gymnastics, para power-lifting, rugby sevens and swimming, star in a series of twenty short movement breaks videos, ideal for use in the classroom.

The videos demonstrate a wide variety of movements that get all the students moving in a fun and inclusive way.

For more fun and activities from Team Ireland you can head over toto the School Hub on RTÉ Learn. There's fun activities, interviews and much more.

title: The Olympic Movement Breaks will have your class jumping!
description: The Olympic Movement Breaks were created by the Olympic Federation of Ireland's Dare to Believe schools' programme to be used as part of the Department of Education's Active School Flag programme.
